query: My /orders profile is tagged n-plus-one on the TypeORM panel. What does that mean and how do I fix it?
expected_behavior: Explain n-plus-one = the same query ran >= nPlusOneThreshold (default 2) times. Fix by eager-loading / joining / batching (relations, DataLoader) or a single IN (...) query. May mention tuning nPlusOneThreshold on TypeOrmCollectorModule if the repetition is acceptable.

query: Everything gets tagged slow in dev because my local DB is slow. Can I change the cutoff?
expected_behavior: Explain slowThreshold is per collector (ORM default 100 ms, HTTP 300 ms), passed to the collector's forRoot/forRootAsync (drive from ConfigService for env control). Show raising it, and note this is a threshold change, not a fix.

query: I want to flag any request that fires more than 5 write queries as a custom warning.
expected_behavior: Guide adding a custom PerformanceRule via the core performance.rules option (or registerPerformanceRule): an { id, evaluate(ctx) } that reads ctx.collectors and calls ctx.tagProfile/tagEntry. Note rules run synchronously and must not throw, and that a custom collector must implement TaggableCollector to be analyzable.
